patterns.value: Deep navy + gold + warm white is the canonical trust palette for premium legal/financial. Gold used ONLY as accent (borders, dividers, stat numbers, eyebrows) — never as fill on large areas. Single-file HTML works well for this archetype.

1px gap + background on wrapper creates clean divider lines between cards without double-borders. Pair with bottom-line ::after reveal-on-hover for a premium feel that doesn't add visual noise at rest.

When photos are forbidden: law/finance sites benefit from geometric SVG emblems (shield+scales), structural line overlays on hero, and decorative framed compositions. More credible than stock photography for B2B audiences.

layout_patterns.insight: Horror Steam landing: hide all nav except single CTA (Wishlist Steam). Bento grid for mechanics with stat callouts. Sticky scroll for atmospheric about section. Developer quote before final CTA humanizes indie project.

component_patterns.insight: Horror buttons use uppercase micro-font 0.06em tracking + blood/ethereal variants; box-shadow drives emotion not background-color alone; ::before gradient adds depth without color change

card-document with ::after corner fold + .is-redacted stamp creates strong narrative impact; card-terminal uses CRT scanlines inline via repeating-linear-gradient; translateY(-3px) hover feels corpse-like but deliberate
0.6875rem / letter-spacing 0.1em / uppercase works at small size in dark bg; 4 semantic variants map to the color palette directly (blood=danger, ethereal=special, rot=environmental)
